title: Elemental Aura Post-Processing Lab
description: The Xbot stands center-stage flanked by a WaterBottle and a ShaderBall, each radiating a distinct elemental aura. The user manipulates a multi-pass post-processing pipeline—toggling bloom, chromatic aberration, vignette, color grading, pixelation, and a custom radial blur—while adjusting per-object emissive glow intensity to craft a cinematic composite.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load Xbot.glb at origin (scale 1), WaterBottle.glb at (-2, 0.5, 0) scaled 8x, and ShaderBall.glb at (2, 0.5, 0) scaled 1.5x. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0, 2, 6) looking at origin. Add an ambient light (0.3) and two directional lights (warm key at upper-left, cool fill at lower-right). Set renderer.toneMapping to ACESFilmicToneMapping with exposure 1.2. Build an EffectComposer with these passes: RenderPass, UnrealBloomPass (threshold 0.4, strength 1.5, radius 0.8), ShaderPass for chromatic aberration (custom uniforms: offset float default 0.003), ShaderPass for vignette (uniforms: intensity 0.8, smoothness 0.4), ShaderPass for color grading (uniforms: saturation 1.0, brightness 1.0, contrast 1.0), ShaderPass for pixelation (uniform: pixelSize default 1.0 meaning off), and a custom ShaderPass for radial blur (uniforms: blurStrength 0.0, center vec2(0.5,0.5)). Create a dat.GUI panel with folders: 'Bloom' containing threshold, strength, radius sliders; 'Chromatic Aberration' with offset slider 0-0.02; 'Vignette' with intensity 0-2 and smoothness 0-1; 'Color Grading' with saturation 0-3, brightness 0-2, contrast 0-2; 'Pixelation' with pixelSize 1-16 step 1; 'Radial Blur' with blurStrength 0-0.1. Add an 'Emissive Glow' folder with sliders for Xbot glow color (via color picker) and intensity 0-3, WaterBottle glow intensity 0-5, and ShaderBall glow intensity 0-5. When glow intensity changes, traverse each model and set emissive and emissiveIntensity on all MeshStandardMaterials found. Animate Xbot using its built-in idle animation via AnimationMixer. Slowly rotate WaterBottle and ShaderBall on Y axis. Add OrbitControls. The user must interact with at least 6 different parameter groups to sculpt the final look. Ensure all shader passes compile and the pipeline renders correctly with proper pass ordering.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomThreshold: number,         // bloom pass threshold value (0-1)
  bloomStrength: number,         // bloom pass strength multiplier
  bloomRadius: number,         // bloom pass radius in pixels
  chromaticOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration offset amount (0-0.02)
  vignetteIntensity: number,         // vignette darkness intensity (0-2)
  vignetteSmoothness: number,         // vignette edge smoothness (0-1)
  colorSaturation: number,         // color grading saturation multiplier (0-3)
  colorBrightness: number,         // color grading brightness multiplier (0-2)
  colorContrast: number,         // color grading contrast multiplier (0-2)
  pixelSize: number,         // pixelation effect size (1=off, higher=more pixelated)
  radialBlurStrength: number,         // radial blur intensity (0-1)
  assetsLoaded: boolean,         // true when all three GLB models are loaded
}
